commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7b0472245434b01b11b3a120acee51b9c22f27b5)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a direct XCP-ng provider with XAPI lifecycle, configuration and CLI integration, documentation, tests, and guarded live and ISO validation tooling.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new provider feature rather than a current-main bug report, and the proposed behavior instead has live after-fix validation through real XCP-ng lifecycle paths.
commands.review_followup: - A maintainer should accept or narrow the new provider contract and security boundary; the only concrete patch cleanup is removal of release-owned changelog entries.

target.title: feat(provider): add XCP-ng provider
target.body:
## Summary

This is a refreshed PR summary for current head `2a1b04f`.

PR #222 now adds a full XCP-ng provider to Crabbox, plus guarded live-smoke and guarded fresh-installer ISO E2E validation. At a high level, Crabbox can now talk directly to XAPI, resolve template/SR/network/host placement, clone and provision a VM, attach per-lease config-drive cloud-init, wait for XCP-ng guest metrics to report a usable IPv4, bootstrap over SSH, and then use the normal Crabbox sync/run/release lifecycle against that lease.

## Capability / boundary snapshot

- Provider name: `xcp-ng`
- Mode: direct-only; no coordinator-managed capacity or broker provisioning
- Targets: `linux` only for normal lease provisioning
- Declared features: `ssh`, `crabbox-sync`, `cleanup`
- Primary workflow: `doctor`, `warmup`, `run`, `ssh`, `stop`, `cleanup`
- Auth model: XAPI credentials from private config / env / secret store only; there is intentionally no `--xcp-ng-password` flag
- Live validation surfaces in this branch:
  - guarded provider smoke for the normal template-clone contract
  - guarded ISO installer E2E harness for fresh Linux installs and provided-answer Windows installs; the generated-answer Windows path is explicitly deferred in this head until it has real live proof

## What this PR adds

### Provider implementation and lifecycle

- Adds `internal/providers/xcpng` as a new provider package with provider registration, backend lifecycle handling, XAPI client logic, cloud-init/config-drive generation, typed provider state, and focused provider tests.
- Implements acquisition and lifecycle handling that:
  - validates provider config;
  - opens a XAPI session;
  - lists existing Crabbox-managed VMs for slug allocation;
  - resolves template, SR, optional network, and optional host placement;
  - clones/copies the template, labels the VM, and provisions it;
  - builds and attaches a FAT16 `CIDATA` config drive;
  - starts the VM;
  - waits for guest metrics to report a non-loopback IPv4;
  - waits for SSH readiness and hands back a normal direct SSH lease target.
- Implements resolve/list/touch/release/cleanup behavior so existing direct-lease command paths work with `xcp-ng`.
- Keeps destructive cleanup restricted to Crabbox-managed XCP-ng resources and removes stored per-lease SSH keys on release.

### XAPI, VM, and disk handling

- Adds a substantial XAPI client covering login/logout, session handling, pool redirects, VM lookup/clone/start/shutdown/destroy, storage and VBD/VDI operations, guest metrics polling, and label management.
- Uses durable VM identity handling and stricter ownership checks so rollback/release/cleanup stay scoped to the VM and config-drive resources Crabbox created.
- Follows `HOST_IS_SLAVE` redirects to the reported pool master for both login-time and post-login redirects.
- Uses `VIF.move`-based network rewiring for configured network placement on real XCP-ng pools.
- Hardens halt/detach/destroy ordering so config-drive and disk cleanup works for already-halted VMs and non-hot-unpluggable VBDs.

### Cloud-init / bootstrap behavior

- Adds XCP-ng-specific cloud-init/config-drive generation in `internal/providers/xcpng/cloudinit.go`.
- Generates a per-lease SSH-enabled payload that:
  - creates the Crabbox SSH user and authorized key;
  - configures the SSH port contract Crabbox expects;
  - prepares the remote work root and cache directories;
  - installs required bootstrap packages with bounded apt retry behavior;
  - marks readiness for the normal Linux bootstrap flow.
- Builds FAT16 config-drive images in-process instead of depending on external tooling.
- Rejects oversized payloads cleanly instead of panicking.

### CLI, config, and command-surface integration

- Extends the config model with `XCPNgConfig` plus YAML/env loading for API URL, username, template/template UUID, SR/SR UUID, network/network UUID, host, user, work root, and insecure TLS.
- Adds the matching non-secret CLI flags and integrates XCP-ng into provider help, config diagnostics, provider backend routing, stop/failure routing args, and server-type derivation.
- Keeps passwords out of argv entirely: there is still no `--xcp-ng-password` flag.
- Validates unsupported provider/target combinations in doctor/provisioning while still allowing existing XCP-ng lease operations when local config has drifted into an unsupported provisioning mode.

### Doctor and docs

- Adds non-mutating XCP-ng doctor support that validates required config, opens a XAPI session, resolves configured placement, lists Crabbox-managed inventory, and reports `mutation=false`.
- Adds the full provider guide at `docs/providers/xcp-ng.md`.
- Updates command docs / feature docs / provider indexes so XCP-ng appears as a first-class supported provider across the docs surface.

### Guarded live smoke tooling

- Adds `scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.sh` as the guarded smoke helper for the normal PR 222 template-clone contract.
- Read-only mode runs `crabbox doctor --provider xcp-ng --json`, writes redacted evidence, and makes no provider mutations.
- Mutating mode is explicitly gated behind `CRABBOX_XCP_NG_LIVE_MUTATE=1`, creates one kept lease, runs a minimal `--no-sync` command, and stops that exact lease.
- Redacts configured pool URLs plus secret-like fields from retained evidence.
- Preserves caller-supplied environment variables over env-file defaults so CI and operator overrides win.

### Guarded ISO E2E install harness

- Adds `internal/providers/xcpng/iso_e2e.go`, `cmd/xcpng-iso-e2e-helper`, `scripts/xcpng-iso-e2e-smoke.sh`, and `scripts/xcpng-iso-e2e-smoke.test.js`.
- Keeps ISO E2E separate from the normal template-clone smoke because it validates fresh blank VM + installer media lifecycle instead of the normal provider contract.
- Read-only mode validates auth/placement/media references and writes redacted evidence without creating XCP-ng resources.
- Linux mutating mode:
  - generates a NoCloud seed ISO with Ubuntu autoinstall data;
  - remasters/imports installer media when given a local ISO path;
  - creates a fresh blank VM plus install disk;
  - boots the installer, waits for first boot, and proves the installed guest over SSH with `printf linux-iso-e2e-ok`;
  - cleans up the VM, disk, and temporary imported media.
- Windows mutating mode in this PR is limited to supplied answer media:
  - expects x86_64/x64 installer media;
  - accepts operator-supplied answer media and reports `source_uncovered` when only guest-metrics readiness is available for that supplied path;
  - explicitly gates the generated-answer media path as `windows_requirements_blocked` until it has real live proof on XCP-ng, instead of claiming support without evidence.
- Exposes stable JSON summary keys (`classification`, `mutation`, `os`, `iso`, `phase`, `cleanup`, `evidence`) and explicit classifications including `read_only_passed`, `linux_install_passed`, `windows_install_passed`, `windows_requirements_blocked`, `source_uncovered`, `environment_blocked`, `resource_cleanup_failed`, and `test_failed`.

## Hardening and follow-up fixes included in this branch

Beyond the base provider, the branch now includes a long tail of review- and mutation-driven fixes, including:

- strict UUID vs `OpaqueRef` handling and durable VM identity selection;
- pool-member redirect handling and fresh-session retry behavior;
- safer disk/config-drive ownership checks and rollback cleanup;
- link-local / unusable guest-IP filtering and clearer readiness timeouts;
- stricter doctor/template/placement validation;
- logout/session cleanup correctness;
- quoted work roots, SSH contract fixes, and bounded package-bootstrap retries;
- live-smoke env-file precedence and cleanup fixes;
- routing-safe stop/failure command generation for XCP-ng;
- URL userinfo stripping/redaction across config display, generated commands, XML-RPC faults, transport errors, upload/import errors, and malformed-endpoint error paths.

## Test coverage added or expanded

This PR now has broad regression coverage across:

- provider registration and help surfaces;
- config parsing, env loading, config show, and secret redaction;
- backend routing, server-type derivation, and provider target validation;
- XCP-ng lifecycle behavior, release handling, cleanup gating, and rollback edge cases;
- XAPI request/response handling, redirect logic, endpoint normalization, guest metrics polling, and error redaction;
- cloud-init/config-drive generation for both normal template-based leases and ISO E2E bootstrap payloads;
- live-smoke shell behavior and redaction rules;
- ISO E2E harness behavior, stable summaries, env precedence, mutation gates, and redaction rules.

Largest new / expanded test surfaces include:

- `internal/providers/xcpng/backend_test.go`
- `internal/providers/xcpng/client_test.go`
- `internal/providers/xcpng/cloudinit_test.go`
- `internal/providers/xcpng/provider_test.go`
- `internal/cli/config_cmd_test.go`
- `internal/cli/provider_backend_test.go`
- `internal/cli/doctor_test.go`
- `internal/cli/run_failure_test.go`
- `internal/cli/profiles_test.go`
- `scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.test.js`
- `scripts/xcpng-iso-e2e-smoke.test.js`

## Live proof already captured in the PR thread

Read-only smoke:

```text
scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.sh --read-only
classification=read_only_doctor_passed
mutation=false
```

Mutating smoke:

```text
CRABBOX_XCP_NG_LIVE_MUTATE=1 scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.sh --mutate
classification=live_smoke_passed
warmup: provisioned lease=<lease> server=<vm-uuid> ip=<redacted-private-ip>
warmup: ready ssh=crabbox@<redacted-private-ip>:22 network=public workroot=/work/crabbox
run: running on <redacted-private-ip> echo xcp-ng-ok
run: xcp-ng-ok
stop: deleted lease=<lease> server=<vm-uuid> name=<smoke-vm-name>
```

Cleanup proof after the smoke:

```text
crabbox list --provider xcp-ng --json
[]
```

Linux mutating ISO E2E live proof:

```json
{
  "classification": "linux_install_passed",
  "phase": "linux_ssh_ok",
  "installer_source": "sr-vdi",
  "answer_iso_source": "generated-config-drive",
  "first_boot_ip": "192.168.1.x"
}
```

Current head `2a1b04f` also removes the remaining macOS-only `hdiutil` dependency from generated ISO media paths.

Windows/generated-answer ISO status:

- live XCP-ng reruns after the Linux proof still did not produce reviewer-grade after-fix proof for the generated-answer auxiliary-media path
- one rerun dropped to the UEFI shell before install, and a later insert attempt surfaced `xenopsd internal error: unexpected disk for devid 51776`
- this head now explicitly defers that generated-answer Windows path from the PR by gating it as `windows_requirements_blocked` until it has real live proof instead of claiming support prematurely

## Verification

Fresh audit reruns at current head `2a1b04f`:

- `go test ./internal/providers/xcpng -count=1`
- `go vet ./...`
- `go test -race ./...`
- `npm run format:check --prefix worker`
- `npm run lint --prefix worker`
- `npm run check --prefix worker`
- `npm test --prefix worker`

Previously run during thread follow-ups:

- `go test -race ./...`
- `go test ./...`
- `go vet ./...`
- `go build -trimpath -o bin/crabbox ./cmd/crabbox`
- `node scripts/build-docs-site.mjs`
- `node --test scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.test.js`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Related

- Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/217
- Follows up on https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/217#issuecomment-4637370969

## Notes

- PR head is `2a1b04f` on `feat/xcp-ng-provider`.
- Live smoke and ISO E2E remain opt-in and require local XCP-ng credentials plus pool/media access.
target.author: coygeek

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

### CLI, config, and command-surface integration

- Extends the config model with `XCPNgConfig` plus YAML/env loading for API URL, username, template/template UUID, SR/SR UUID, network/network UUID, host, user, work root, and insecure TLS.
- Adds the matching non-secret CLI flags and integrates XCP-ng into provider help, config diagnostics, provider backend routing, stop/failure routing args, and server-type derivation.
- Keeps passwords out of argv entirely: there is still no `--xcp-ng-password` flag.
- Validates unsupported provider/target combinations in doctor/provisioning while still allowing existing XCP-ng lease operations when local config has drifted into an unsupported provisioning mode.

### Doctor and docs

- Adds non-mutating XCP-ng doctor support that validates required config, opens a XAPI session, resolves configured placement, lists Crabbox-managed inventory, and reports `mutation=false`.
- Adds the full provider guide at `docs/providers/xcp-ng.md`.
- Updates command docs / feature docs / provider indexes so XCP-ng appears as a first-class supported provider across the docs surface.

### Guarded live smoke tooling

- Adds `scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.sh` as the guarded smoke helper for the normal PR 222 template-clone contract.
- Read-only mode runs `crabbox doctor --provider xcp-ng --json`, writes redacted evidence, and makes no provider mutations.
- Mutating mode is explicitly gated behind `CRABBOX_XCP_NG_LIVE_MUTATE=1`, creates one kept lease, runs a minimal `--no-sync` command, and stops that exact lease.
- Redacts configured pool URLs plus secret-like fields from retained evidence.
- Preserves caller-supplied environment variables over env-file defaults so CI and operator overrides win.

### Guarded ISO E2E install harness

- Adds `internal/providers/xcpng/iso_e2e.go`, `cmd/xcpng-iso-e2e-helper`, `scripts/xcpng-iso-e2e-smoke.sh`, and `scripts/xcpng-iso-e2e-smoke.test.js`.
- Keeps ISO E2E separate from the normal template-clone smoke because it validates fresh blank VM + installer media lifecycle instead of the normal provider contract.
- Read-only mode validates auth/placement/media references and writes redacted evidence without creating XCP-ng resources.
- Linux mutating mode:
  - generates a NoCloud seed ISO with Ubuntu autoinstall data;
  - remasters/imports installer media when given a local ISO path;
  - creates a fresh blank VM plus install disk;
  - boots the installer, waits for first boot, and proves the installed guest over SSH with `printf linux-iso-e2e-ok`;
  - cleans up the VM, disk, and temporary imported media.
- Windows mutating mode in this PR is limited to supplied answer media:
  - expects x86_64/x64 installer media;
  - accepts operator-supplied answer media and reports `source_uncovered` when only guest-metrics readiness is available for that supplied path;
  - explicitly gates the generated-answer media path as `windows_requirements_blocked` until it has real live proof on XCP-ng, instead of claiming support without evidence.
- Exposes stable JSON summary keys (`classification`, `mutation`, `os`, `iso`, `phase`, `cleanup`, `evidence`) and explicit classifications including `read_only_passed`, `linux_install_passed`, `windows_install_passed`, `windows_requirements_blocked`, `source_uncovered`, `environment_blocked`, `resource_cleanup_failed`, and `test_failed`.

## Hardening and follow-up fixes included in this branch

Beyond the base provider, the branch now includes a long tail of review- and mutation-driven fixes, including:

- strict UUID vs `OpaqueRef` handling and durable VM identity selection;
- pool-member redirect handling and fresh-session retry behavior;
- safer disk/config-drive ownership checks and rollback cleanup;
- link-local / unusable guest-IP filtering and clearer readiness timeouts;
- stricter doctor/template/placement validation;
- logout/session cleanup correctness;
- quoted work roots, SSH contract fixes, and bounded package-bootstrap retries;
- live-smoke env-file precedence and cleanup fixes;
- routing-safe stop/failure command generation for XCP-ng;
- URL userinfo stripping/redaction across config display, generated commands, XML-RPC faults, transport errors, upload/import errors, and malformed-endpoint error paths.

## Test coverage added or expanded

This PR now has broad regression coverage across:

- provider registration and help surfaces;
- config parsing, env loading, config show, and secret redaction;
- backend routing, server-type derivation, and provider target validation;
- XCP-ng lifecycle behavior, release handling, cleanup gating, and rollback edge cases;
- XAPI request/response handling, redirect logic, endpoint normalization, guest metrics polling, and error redaction;
- cloud-init/config-drive generation for both normal template-based leases and ISO E2E bootstrap payloads;
- live-smoke shell behavior and redaction rules;
- ISO E2E harness behavior, stable summaries, env precedence, mutation gates, and redaction rules.

Largest new / expanded test surfaces include:

- `internal/providers/xcpng/backend_test.go`
- `internal/providers/xcpng/client_test.go`
- `internal/providers/xcpng/cloudinit_test.go`
- `internal/providers/xcpng/provider_test.go`
- `internal/cli/config_cmd_test.go`
- `internal/cli/provider_backend_test.go`
- `internal/cli/doctor_test.go`
- `internal/cli/run_failure_test.go`
- `internal/cli/profiles_test.go`
- `scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.test.js`
- `scripts/xcpng-iso-e2e-smoke.test.js`

## Live proof already captured in the PR thread

Read-only smoke:

```text
scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.sh --read-only
classification=read_only_doctor_passed
mutation=false
```

Mutating smoke:

```text
CRABBOX_XCP_NG_LIVE_MUTATE=1 scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.sh --mutate
classification=live_smoke_passed
warmup: provisioned lease=<lease> server=<vm-uuid> ip=<redacted-private-ip>
warmup: ready ssh=crabbox@<redacted-private-ip>:22 network=public workroot=/work/crabbox
run: running on <redacted-private-ip> echo xcp-ng-ok
run: xcp-ng-ok
stop: deleted lease=<lease> server=<vm-uuid> name=<smoke-vm-name>
```

Cleanup proof after the smoke:

```text
crabbox list --provider xcp-ng --json
[]
```

Linux mutating ISO E2E live proof:

```json
{
  "classification": "linux_install_passed",
  "phase": "linux_ssh_ok",
  "installer_source": "sr-vdi",
  "answer_iso_source": "generated-config-drive",
  "first_boot_ip": "192.168.1.x"
}
```

Current head `2a1b04f` also removes the remaining macOS-only `hdiutil` dependency from generated ISO media paths.

Windows/generated-answer ISO status:

- live XCP-ng reruns after the Linux proof still did not produce reviewer-grade after-fix proof for the generated-answer auxiliary-media path
- one rerun dropped to the UEFI shell before install, and a later insert attempt surfaced `xenopsd internal error: unexpected disk for devid 51776`
- this head now explicitly defers that generated-answer Windows path from the PR by gating it as `windows_requirements_blocked` until it has real live proof instead of claiming support prematurely

## Verification

Fresh audit reruns at current head `2a1b04f`:

- `go test ./internal/providers/xcpng -count=1`
- `go vet ./...`
- `go test -race ./...`
- `npm run format:check --prefix worker`
- `npm run lint --prefix worker`
- `npm run check --prefix worker`
- `npm test --prefix worker`

Previously run during thread follow-ups:

- `go test -race ./...`
- `go test ./...`
- `go vet ./...`
- `go build -trimpath -o bin/crabbox ./cmd/crabbox`
- `node scripts/build-docs-site.mjs`
- `node --test scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.test.js`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Related

- Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/217
- Follows up on https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/217#issuecomment-4637370969

## Notes

- PR head is `2a1b04f` on `feat/xcp-ng-provider`.
- Live smoke and ISO E2E remain opt-in and require local XCP-ng credentials plus pool/media access.
